SEARCH
Package com.comp2042.model.board

Class SimpleBoard

java.lang.Object
com.comp2042.model.board.SimpleBoard
All Implemented Interfaces:
Board
public class SimpleBoard extends Object implements Board
Core Tetris board implementation managing game state and brick movement. Handles board grid, falling brick, collision detection, row clearing, and scoring. Supports configurable brick generators (7-bag or pure random) via GameSettings.
Author:
Dong, Jia.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    SimpleBoard(int width, int height)
    Constructs a SimpleBoard with specified dimensions.

    Description

    addGarbageLine()
    Adds a garbage line to the bottom of the board.

    clearRows()
    Checks the board for completed rows, removes them, shifts the remaining rows down, calculates the score bonus, updates the internal board matrix, and awards the score.

    Creates a memento containing the current game state.

    Gets the currently held brick.

    Gets the total number of lines cleared in the current game.

    Gets the current view data (brick shape, position, next brick shape).

    Instantly drops the currently falling brick to the bottom of the board.

    Swaps the current brick with the held brick.

    Moves the falling brick one position down.

    Attempts to move the currently falling brick one position to the left.
    boolean

    Attempts to move the currently falling brick one position to the right.

    Resets the board state for a new game.

    rotateLeftBrick()
    Attempts to rotate the currently falling brick clockwise.
    boolean
    rotateRightBrick()
    Attempts to rotate the currently falling brick counterclockwise.

  • Constructor Details

    • SimpleBoard

      public SimpleBoard(int width, int height)
      Constructs a SimpleBoard with specified dimensions. Initializes board components based on GameSettings (generator type).
      Parameters:
      width - Board width (must be positive)
      height - Board height (must be positive)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if dimensions invalid

  • Method Details

    • moveBrickDown

      public boolean moveBrickDown()
      Moves the falling brick one position down.
      Specified by:
      moveBrickDown in interface Board
      Returns:
      true if successful, false if collision (brick lands)

    • hardDropBrick

      public int hardDropBrick()
      Instantly drops the currently falling brick to the bottom of the board. This is the hard drop functionality that moves the brick as far down as possible.
      Specified by:
      hardDropBrick in interface Board
      Returns:
      the number of positions the brick was moved down

    • moveBrickLeft

      public boolean moveBrickLeft()
      Attempts to move the currently falling brick one position to the left. Checks for collisions with the left wall or other placed bricks.
      Specified by:
      moveBrickLeft in interface Board
      Returns:
      true if the move was successful, false if a collision occurred

    • moveBrickRight

      public boolean moveBrickRight()
      Attempts to move the currently falling brick one position to the right. Checks for collisions with the right wall or other placed bricks.
      Specified by:
      moveBrickRight in interface Board
      Returns:
      true if the move was successful, false if a collision occurred

    • rotateLeftBrick

      public boolean rotateLeftBrick()
      Attempts to rotate the currently falling brick clockwise. Calculates the potential next shape and checks for collisions in the new orientation.
      Specified by:
      rotateLeftBrick in interface Board
      Returns:
      true if the rotation was successful, false if a collision occurred

    • rotateRightBrick

      public boolean rotateRightBrick()
      Attempts to rotate the currently falling brick counterclockwise. Calculates the potential previous shape and checks for collisions in the new orientation.
      Specified by:
      rotateRightBrick in interface Board
      Returns:
      true if the rotation was successful, false if a collision occurred.

    • createNewBrick

      public boolean createNewBrick()
      Creates a new brick for the board. Gets a brick from the generator, sets it in the rotator, positions it at the top/middle, and checks if this initial position causes a collision (indicating game over).
      Specified by:
      createNewBrick in interface Board
      Returns:
      true if a collision occurs immediately (game over), false otherwise

    • getBoardMatrix

      public int[][] getBoardMatrix()
      Gets the current state of the board grid. Returns a copy to prevent external modification.
      Specified by:
      getBoardMatrix in interface Board
      Returns:
      a copy of the current board matrix

    • getViewData

      public ViewData getViewData()
      Gets the current view data (brick shape, position, next brick shape).
      Specified by:
      getViewData in interface Board
      Returns:
      a ViewData object containing the necessary information for the GUI

    • mergeBrickToBackground

      public void mergeBrickToBackground()
      Merges the currently falling brick permanently onto the board grid. This happens when the brick lands after moving down.
      Specified by:
      mergeBrickToBackground in interface Board

    • clearRows

      public ClearRow clearRows()
      Checks the board for completed rows, removes them, shifts the remaining rows down, calculates the score bonus, updates the internal board matrix, and awards the score.
      Specified by:
      clearRows in interface Board
      Returns:
      a ClearRow object containing details about the cleared lines

    • getScore

      public Score getScore()
      Gets the current score object.
      Specified by:
      getScore in interface Board
      Returns:
      the Score instance

    • getTotalLinesCleared

      public int getTotalLinesCleared()
      Gets the total number of lines cleared in the current game.
      Specified by:
      getTotalLinesCleared in interface Board
      Returns:
      The total lines cleared.

    • holdBrick

      public boolean holdBrick()
      Swaps the current brick with the held brick.
      Specified by:
      holdBrick in interface Board
      Returns:
      true if hold operation was successful

    • getHeldBrick

      public Brick getHeldBrick()
      Gets the currently held brick.
      Specified by:
      getHeldBrick in interface Board
      Returns:
      the held brick, or null if no brick is held

    • getNextBrick

      public int[][] getNextBrick()
      Gets the next brick shape data.
      Returns:
      the next brick shape matrix

    • addGarbageLine

      public boolean addGarbageLine()
      Adds a garbage line to the bottom of the board. A garbage line is a full row with one random hole. This is used for Two-player mode attacks.
      Specified by:
      addGarbageLine in interface Board
      Returns:
      true if adding the garbage line causes game over

    • removeGarbageLines

      public int removeGarbageLines(int linesToRemove)
      Removes garbage lines from the bottom of the board. Scans for lines containing only garbage blocks (and empty space) and removes them.
      Specified by:
      removeGarbageLines in interface Board
      Parameters:
      linesToRemove - the number of garbage lines to remove
      Returns:
      the actual number of lines removed

    • newGame

      public void newGame()
      Resets the board state for a new game. Clears the board matrix, resets the score, and creates the first new brick.
      Specified by:
      newGame in interface Board

    • getGhostBrickY

      public int getGhostBrickY()
      Description copied from interface: Board
      Calculates the ghost brick position (where the brick would land if dropped straight down). The ghost brick has the same shape and x-position as the current brick, but with the y-position adjusted to show where it would land.
      Specified by:
      getGhostBrickY in interface Board
      Returns:
      the y-coordinate (row) where the ghost brick would land, or -1 if calculation fails

    • createMemento

      public GameStateMemento createMemento()
      Creates a memento containing the current game state. Implements the Memento Pattern's Originator role.
      Returns:
      a GameStateMemento containing the current game state

    • restoreFromMemento

      public void restoreFromMemento(GameStateMemento memento)
      Restores the game state from a memento. Implements the Memento Pattern's Originator role.
      Parameters:
      memento - the GameStateMemento to restore from
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if memento is null or dimensions don't match
